moniker: Cork missing rigging

invariant.name: missing rigging atoms on the corkline → cannot prove → yellow
invariant.quote: MISSING: there is insufficient rigging trie data to establish whether the trie satisfies the hoist condition. Yellow — missing information, not a provable failure.

moniker: Cork previous invalid (green)

invariant.name: an invalid predecessor that is not on the rig's walked corkline must not poison a green rig
invariant.quote: The (cork)line K is checked (and recorded in the linestore) by following prev(s) from k5 to k0. The traversal walks the corkline specified by the inputs; atoms outside that walk are not part of the check.

moniker: Cork previous invalid

invariant.name: invalid prev pointer beyond z_a → verifier sees a complete corkline within its view → hoist MISSING (yellow)
invariant.quote: find_line_start stops at the last valid twist when it hits an invalid prev pointer, so z_a is that twist. construct_line(z_a, z_w) reaches z_a cleanly — the bad pointer is *beyond* the verifier's view of the corkline. With a complete-looking corkline but no matching k1 entry, §9.5 hoist MISSING applies (insufficient trie data) → yellow.

moniker: Cork reqsat failure (formerly tested strict whole-corkline validation)

invariant.name: the rig walks cleanly; a bad-reqsat twist sitting on the corkline but not consulted as a hoist must not flip a green rig to red
invariant.quote: Spec §5.1 defines valid(t) = satisfies(t.prev.reqs, t.sats) — purely local. A rig's verdict must depend only on the twists the walk actually touches; otherwise simply adding loose unrelated twists to the file could flip a passing rig to failing, which would be unsound (the rig itself didn't change). Rustoda now validates reqsat only on hoists and meets the walk accepts.

moniker: Corkline incomplete early (red)

invariant.name: incomplete corkline that fails the walk-back invariant
invariant.quote: MISMATCH: The corkline traversal reached NULL rather than the specified earliest twist. A rig checker may treat an atomic error as a yellow error, and/or a shape error as red — implementations choose where to draw the line.

moniker: Corkline incomplete late

invariant.name: corkline ends before reaching the hoist position → rig UNKNOWN (yellow) since the verifier can't prove the corkline is incapable
invariant.quote: Spec §9.6 lists 'corkline does not support this rig's leadline' as rig INVALID, but that requires *provable* incapacity. A truncated-but-internally-consistent corkline is indistinguishable from 'this is the actual corkline and no hoist exists' — both look like absence of evidence. §9.1.3 UNKNOWN applies: it is unclear how to interpret the data → yellow. Red would require external knowledge that the corkline 'should' be longer.

It includes an assortment of tether transitions and other such elements that are expected to be relatively commonplace in actual implemented rigs.

moniker: Two different leads claim the same (fastener, hoist) entry on the topline rigging trie

invariant.name: pairtrie key uniqueness is enforced at parse time; the asserted 'two leads on the same entry' contradiction cannot exist in a parseable pairtrie
invariant.quote: Pairtrie shape forbids duplicate keys (spec §3). For two different leads to claim 'the same entry', their distinct shielded keys would have to collide — which is a SHA-256 collision argument, not a verifier-checkable invariant on well-formed bytes. The asserted condition isn't actually present in this file's parseable trie content.

moniker: Rig half-hitch Test : Invalid half-hitch, corkline twist missing

invariant.name: missing corkline twist atom → verifier sees a truncated corkline → hoist MISSING (yellow)
invariant.quote: When a corkline atom is absent from the file, find_line_start stops at the last bundled twist. Within the corkline the verifier *can* see, no twist has the lead's k1 entry — §9.5 hoist MISSING (insufficient trie data) → yellow. The fact that the rig 'claims' an earlier twist that isn't bundled is a MISSING condition (atom not provided), not a MISMATCH.

moniker: Rig half-hitch Test : Invalid half-hitch, footline prev gap before lead

invariant.name: footline must connect lead to meet via prev pointers without gaps
invariant.quote: hitch.footline = [hitch.lead, ..., hitch.meet]. A prev gap means the terminal twist of the lead-footline does not match the lead → MISMATCH.

moniker: Rig half-hitch Test : Invalid half-hitch, no matching values in topline rigging for s(lead) and ss(lead)

invariant.name: shielded primary and verification entries must agree
invariant.quote: hoist MISMATCH explicit case: rig[shield(lead, (shield(lead, I(lead))))] != shield(lead, (rig[shield(lead, I(lead))])). The s² verification entry must equal S(meet) where meet is the s-entry value; if the two values disagree the hoist is provably incapable of hoisting the lead → MISMATCH/red.

moniker: Rig half-hitch Test : Invalid half-hitch, no s(lead) entry in top line

invariant.name: no s(lead) entry on any topline twist → hoist MISSING (yellow), not MISMATCH
invariant.quote: hoist MISSING: there is insufficient trie data to establish whether the provided lead is a key. Hoist MISMATCH is reserved for cases where entries *exist* but are provably wrong (null proof, value=key, or s/s² disagreement) — absence of the primary shielded entry on every topline twist means no candidate hoist exists at all → MISSING, yellow.

moniker: Rig half-hitch Test : Invalid half-hitch, no ss(lead) entry in top line

invariant.name: no s²(lead) verification entry → hoist MISSING (yellow), not MISMATCH
invariant.quote: hoist MISSING: there is insufficient trie data to establish whether the provided lead is a key. The MISMATCH cases listed in §9.5 hoist all require entries to be present and provably wrong (e.g. s²-entry disagrees with s(meet)); a *missing* s² verification entry is the absence of evidence, classified MISSING/yellow.

moniker: Rig half-hitch Test : Invalid half-hitch, hoist points to a non-fast meet

invariant.name: hitch.meet must be a fast twist — meet INVALID if its tether is NULL
invariant.quote: hitch.lead = fastPrev(hitch.meet) — the meet is itself fast. The meet's tether is NULL → meet INVALID (red).

moniker: Rig half-hitch Test : Invalid half-hitch, the values of the hoist's rigging trie are the same as its keys.

invariant.name: front-running defense: rig[shield(lead, I(lead))] = shield(lead, I(lead)) is a MISMATCH per §9.5
invariant.quote: hoist MISMATCH explicit case: 'rig[shield(lead, I(lead))] = shield(lead, I(lead))' — the trie value equalling its own shielded key is the spec's null proof for a non-hoist. If this is the only candidate on the topline, the rig is provably wrong about supporting the lead → MISMATCH/red.

moniker: Rig half-hitch Test : Yellow half-hitch, lead tether is missing.

invariant.name: missing lead.teth atom → MISSING (yellow), not INVALID
invariant.quote: MISSING: There is no provided atom whose identifier matches the lead's tether. Spec §9.1.3: 'MISSING indicates that a piece of required information was not provided' (yellow).

moniker: Rig half-hitch Test : Invalid half-hitch, lead tether is not a twist.

invariant.name: hitch.lead.teth must have basic-twist shape
invariant.quote: teth: twist, NULL, or UNIT ... The lead's tether has a known non-twist shape → INVALID.

moniker: Rig half-hitch Test : Invalid half-hitch, lead tether null.

invariant.name: focus is the only fast twist on its line; corkline has no hoist for it → hoist MISSING (yellow)
invariant.quote: The legacy red came from a verifier bug that treated the focus's line genesis (a loose twist) as the lead, which then reported 'lead INVALID, lead tether is NULL'. Per spec the lead is the earliest fast twist on the leadline (here, the focus itself), and the actual diagnostic is that the corkline simply doesn't carry a hoist for this lead — MISSING is yellow per §9.1.3.

moniker: Rig half-hitch Test : Invalid half-hitch, lead tether is a symbol.

invariant.name: hitch.lead.teth must be a twist, not a symbol
invariant.quote: Symbols, like the NULL and UNIT hashes, are complete atoms unto themselves ... they have no matching preimage. The lead's tether has a known non-twist shape → INVALID.

moniker: Rig half-hitch Test : Valid half-hitch, lead is root (no prev)

invariant.name: lead at root of a line (prev = NULL) is a valid hitch lead
invariant.quote: prev: twist or NULL. A NULL prev marks the start of a line; the lead can still be fast (non-NULL teth) and serve as a hitch lead.

moniker: Rig half-hitch Test : Valid half-hitch, the values of the hoist's rigging trie are the same as its keys, but a subsequent twist is a valid hoist.

invariant.name: front-running null-proof on one twist must not reject the line if a later twist is a valid hoist
invariant.quote: twist.rigs[S(hitch, I(hitch.lead))] = S(hitch, I(hitch.lead)) is one of the null-proofs that no twist between fastener and hoist contains a matching shielded entry — but a later twist on the topline can still be the real hoist.

moniker: Rig half-hitch Test : Valid half-hitch, lead shield non-null arb packet.

invariant.name: lead.shld may be a non-NULL arb packet
invariant.quote: shld: arb or NULL ... we impose the requirement of being an arb shape on lead.shld. A non-NULL arb shield is the normal case; shield(twist, data) = hash(I(twist))(C(twist.shld) | data).

moniker: Rig half-hitch Test : Valid half-hitch, lead shield null.

invariant.name: lead.shld may be NULL; shield function degrades to plain hash
invariant.quote: If twist.shld is NULL the above devolves to shield(twist, data) = hash(I(twist))(data). Note that zero-length packets are a shape error, so setting twist.shld to NULL is the only way to achieve this version.

moniker: Rig half-hitch Test : Invalid half-hitch, wrong non-matching values in hoist pairtrie.

invariant.name: hoist trie entries exist but the verification check (s²/s(meet)) fails → MISMATCH
invariant.quote: hoist MISMATCH explicit case: rig[shield(lead, (shield(lead, I(lead))))] != shield(lead, (rig[shield(lead, I(lead))])). Wrong values in an existing entry mean the trie is provably incapable of hoisting the lead → MISMATCH/red.

moniker: Rig half-hitch Test : Invalid half-hitch, wrong shield used for hoist.

invariant.name: wrong shield value used to compute rig keys → hoist MISSING (indistinguishable from absent entry)
invariant.quote: S(hitch)(data) = shield(hitch.lead, data) = hash(I(lead))(C(lead.shld) | data). If the rig was constructed with a different shield value, the verifier's computed k1 = shield(lead, I(lead)) will not match any entry on the topline — indistinguishable from 'no s(lead) entry at all' from the verifier's perspective. §9.5 hoist MISSING applies: insufficient trie data to establish whether the lead is a key → yellow.

moniker: Lead's prev set to NULL — footline walk cannot reach genesis

invariant.name: the focus's rig verifies; the asserted lead.prev=NULL applies to a non-leadline twist
invariant.quote: The 'lead's prev set to NULL' applies to a twist that isn't the focus's f_a. The focus's actual leadline walks cleanly; spec §7.3 traversal isn't affected.

moniker: Post's prev chain diverges to a different line

invariant.name: the post twist must lie on the leadline; a prev that points to a different line is a provable composition error
invariant.quote: Splicing allows a rig to be extended in time ... spliceHitch.post == a2.

moniker: Post twist has teth overridden to NULL (workshop build; possible duplicate of hitch_meet_tether_null)

invariant.name: spec §7.3 extend_line treats a missing post-rig as a no-op; the rig walks cleanly when the actual leadline is well-formed
invariant.quote: Workshop's red came from line-genesis-as-lead bug. The focus line has two fast twists with a valid hitch; the 'post' the workshop refers to is on a different line and is not consulted as part of the focus's verification per the spec algorithm.

moniker: Full hitch with post (workshop build; possible duplicate of hitch_valid_basic_splice)

invariant.name: splicing composes two leadline-length-1 rigs into a longer leadline
invariant.quote: Splicing allows a rig to be extended in time.

moniker: Extra fast twist between lead and meet (compiled from workshop tests/toda-graph)

invariant.name: a hitch's footline must be a single fast segment: only the lead and meet are fast, all intermediate twists are loose
invariant.quote: left.leadline == [a0, ..., a1], where a0 and a1 are consecutive fast twists, with any number of loose twists between them. An extra fast twist between lead and meet is invalid information about the rig structure.

moniker: Rig hitch Test : Hoist rigs atom missing

invariant.name: missing hoist.rigs atom → MISSING (yellow), not INVALID
invariant.quote: MISSING: there is insufficient rigging trie data to establish whether the trie satisfies the hoist condition. Yellow per §9.1.3 'MISSING indicates that a piece of required information was not provided'.

moniker: Rig hitch Test : Meet tether null

invariant.name: hitch.meet must be fast (non-NULL teth)
invariant.quote: hitch.lead = fastPrev(hitch.meet). A NULL meet.teth means the meet is loose and can't be the lead's next-fast → meet INVALID/red.

moniker: Rig hitch Test : Splice post no entry for lead

invariant.name: missing post-rig is NOT itself an error per spec §7.3 (extend_line is a no-op when post.rig[lead] != hoist)
invariant.quote: if post.rig[lead] = hoist: ... linestore.extend(meet, extension). The extend_line function silently does nothing when the equality fails. Reclassified green in rustoda 2026-05-18; the legacy red label was a misreading of the algorithm.

moniker: Rig hitch Test : Splice post wrong hoist

invariant.name: splice post-key value must match the actual hoist
invariant.quote: spliceHitch.post == a2 ... post.rigs[lead] = hoist. If the post entry exists but points at a different twist than the verified hoist, that's a MISMATCH/red.

moniker: Rig hitch Test : Valid basic splice

invariant.name: splicing composes two leadline-length-1 rigs into a longer leadline
invariant.quote: Splicing allows a rig to be extended in time ... spliceHitch.lead == a0 and spliceHitch.meet = a1 ... right.leadline = [a1, ..., an] ... spliceHitch.post == a2.

moniker: Invalid rigging (green - subsequent twist valid)

invariant.name: an invalid rigging entry on one topline twist does not poison the line if a later twist is a valid hoist
invariant.quote: hitch.hoist is the first successor of hitch.fastener to hoist hitch.lead. Earlier topline twists with malformed rigs simply don't qualify as the hoist; the first valid one wins.

moniker: Invalid shielding (green - subsequent twist valid)

invariant.name: a malformed shielded entry on an earlier topline twist does not block a later valid hoist
invariant.quote: twist.rigs[S(hitch, I(hitch.lead))] is not present in the trie ... is one of several null-proofs that earlier twists don't equivocate on the hoist. Implementations should skip malformed earlier entries rather than rejecting the rig outright.

moniker: Lash succession, missing previous

invariant.name: lashing succession walk: missing prev atom is MISSING (yellow)
invariant.quote: fast-tether: the line connecting the tether of the lead to the fast tether. A missing prev along that succession walk → information not provided → yellow per §9.1.3.

moniker: Lash succession, no fast twist

invariant.name: lashing fast-tether: walk back must reach a fast twist, else MISMATCH
invariant.quote: MISMATCH: The tether and all its predecessors back to NULL are loose. Lashing requires a fast tether to authorise the line; an entirely loose walk fails red.

moniker: Lash succession reqsat failure (formerly tested whole-segment validation)

invariant.name: the rig walks cleanly; a bad-reqsat twist on a lashing line but not consulted as a hoist/meet must not flip the rig red
invariant.quote: Spec §5.1's valid(t) is purely local. The rig walk only needs each twist it actively touches (hoists, meets) to satisfy its predecessor's reqs; bad reqsat on untouched intermediate twists is irrelevant — otherwise the verdict would depend on arbitrary file contents rather than the rig structure itself.

moniker: Lashed non-colinear

invariant.name: lashed sub-rigs whose toplines aren't colinear with the parent leadline violate §7.2
invariant.quote: bottom.topline is a subline of top.leadline. Lashing requires the bottom half-hitch's topline to lie within the top rig's leadline. Non-colinear lashings fail.

js.detail: Conflicting successors. This packet store does not support conflicting successors: 412dfc3525c252569c7fafaef9bc2abcfe52a8c88ce8a00cc7ba1bcb49dcb772bb vs 419060d492b5574a19d3f764f15216260fccada25c89412019536d973bab76c40d

moniker: Lead.shld atom has hashes shape instead of arb (hand-built)

invariant.name: lead.shld must have arb shape (0x60) or be NULL
invariant.quote: we impose the requirement of being an arb shape on lead.shld. Setting it to a shape other than arb results in an issue of INVALID in the lead's status.

moniker: Meets do not match

invariant.name: shielded meet retrieved from hoist.rigs must equal the actual meet
invariant.quote: hitch.hoist.rigs[S(hitch, I(hitch.lead))] = I(hitch.meet). When the retrieved meet does not match the lead's actual fast successor, the rig structure is provably invalid → red.

moniker: Missing rigging

invariant.name: missing rigs atom on the topline → MISSING (yellow)
invariant.quote: MISSING: there is insufficient rigging trie data to establish whether the trie satisfies the hoist condition. Information not provided → yellow.

moniker: Missing shield

invariant.name: missing lead.shld atom → MISSING (yellow), trusted-intermediary may still verify
invariant.quote: MISSING: There is no provided atom whose identifier matches the lead's tether (also applies to lead.shld). Without the shield bytes the µ retrieval can't be computed → MISSING/yellow.

js.detail: The shield for twist 4137f3867e1fd4af828a2932ff3ea99e43c8743909a511da8bc48a17bfd6d8404f is missing

moniker: Multi-level rig: 3 lines, 3 hitches (lash + 2-splice on abject); see also api-valid-lashed-rig

invariant.name: a partially lashed leadline can resume on the corkline via a second splice
invariant.quote: lashed and spliced compositions are both rigs.

moniker: Multiple hoists (green)

invariant.name: the first valid hoist on the topline supports the rig (later candidates are ignored)
invariant.quote: hitch.hoist is the first successor of hitch.fastener to hoist hitch.lead. If multiple twists on the topline could serve as hoists, the earliest one wins and the line is green.

moniker: Nested lashing inside a splice (hand-written)

invariant.name: lashed sub-rigs may be nested inside an outer splice/hitch — §7.3 traversal must recurse correctly
invariant.quote: A rig is constructed from simpler rigs, using the operations we call splicing and lashing. The simplest rigs (i.e. the inductive/recursive base case) are half-hitches. Lashing and splicing compose; the traversal must recurse through intermediate hitch toplines and pop back out to the parent rig.

moniker: Post rigging missing post key

invariant.name: missing post-rig is NOT itself an error per spec §7.3 (extend_line is a no-op when post.rig[lead] != hoist)
invariant.quote: if post.rig[lead] = hoist: extension = [post] ... linestore.extend(meet, extension). The extend_line function silently does nothing when the equality fails — a missing post-rig is not a hard failure. Half-hitches (§7 base case) have no post by definition. Reclassified green in rustoda 2026-05-18; the legacy red label was a misreading of the algorithm.

moniker: R106: lead twist.body slot points to an arb-shape atom (twist.body INVALID)

invariant.name: twist atom's body slot must reference a basic-body-shape (0x49) atom; any other known shape is provably invalid
invariant.spec: §4.1 Basic Twist Shape p. 11; §3.1 Shape Errors p. 10
invariant.quote: body.shape != 0x49 → twist.body INVALID. The rig checker resolves the body identifier and validates its shape; an arb (0x60) atom in place of a body is a structural failure detectable from atoms alone.

moniker: R106: lead twist.body slot points to a hashes-shape atom (twist.body INVALID)

invariant.name: twist atom's body slot must reference a basic-body-shape (0x49) atom; any other known shape is provably invalid
invariant.spec: §4.1 Basic Twist Shape p. 11; §3.1 Shape Errors p. 10
invariant.quote: body.shape != 0x49 → twist.body INVALID. A hashes-shape (0x61) atom in the body slot is provably non-conforming.

moniker: R104: lead twist.body slot points to a twist-shape atom (rustoda emits yellow; spec says twist.body INVALID/red)

invariant.name: twist atom's body slot must reference a basic-body-shape (0x49) atom; a twist-shape (0x48) atom in that slot is structurally wrong per spec
invariant.spec: §4.1 Basic Twist Shape p. 11; §3.1 Shape Errors p. 10
invariant.quote: body.shape = 0x00 - 0x48 → twist.body INVALID. The body slot is constrained to 0x49; any other shape (including the twist shape 0x48 itself) fails the basic twist shape requirement.

moniker: R269: lead.teth shape byte 0x40 (nospec lower range) → lead UNKNOWN

invariant.name: lead.teth has an unrecognized shape byte; the rig checker cannot classify it (lead UNKNOWN, yellow)
invariant.spec: §2.1 Identifiers p. 5 (unrecognized shape bytes are nospec); §9.5 lead UNKNOWN
invariant.quote: lead UNKNOWN: the lead's tether has an unrecognized shape. Shape byte 0x40 is in a nospec range — the checker cannot determine whether it is a twist.

moniker: R270: lead.teth is a body-shape atom (lead INVALID)

invariant.name: lead.teth must be a twist, NULL, or UNIT; a known non-twist atom is provably wrong
invariant.spec: §4.2 Basic Body Shape p. 12 (teth must be twist, NULL, or UNIT); §9.5 lead INVALID
invariant.quote: lead INVALID: the lead's tether has a known non-twist shape. A teth atom of known shape (body, not twist) is structurally invalid for the slot.

moniker: R271: lead.teth shape byte 0x4a (nospec mid range) → lead UNKNOWN

invariant.name: lead.teth has an unrecognized shape byte; the rig checker cannot classify it (lead UNKNOWN, yellow)
invariant.spec: §2.1 Identifiers p. 5 (unrecognized shape bytes are nospec); §9.5 lead UNKNOWN
invariant.quote: lead UNKNOWN: the lead's tether has an unrecognized shape. Shape byte 0x4a is in a nospec range — the checker cannot determine whether it is a twist.

moniker: R272: lead.teth is an arb-shape atom (lead INVALID)

invariant.name: lead.teth must be a twist, NULL, or UNIT; a known non-twist atom is provably wrong
invariant.spec: §4.2 Basic Body Shape p. 12 (teth must be twist, NULL, or UNIT); §9.5 lead INVALID
invariant.quote: lead INVALID: the lead's tether has a known non-twist shape. A teth atom of known shape (arb, not twist) is structurally invalid for the slot.

moniker: R272: lead.teth is a hashes-shape atom (lead INVALID)

invariant.name: lead.teth must be a twist, NULL, or UNIT; a known non-twist atom is provably wrong
invariant.spec: §4.2 Basic Body Shape p. 12 (teth must be twist, NULL, or UNIT); §9.5 lead INVALID
invariant.quote: lead INVALID: the lead's tether has a known non-twist shape. A teth atom of known shape (hashes, not twist) is structurally invalid for the slot.

moniker: R272: lead.teth is a pairtrie-shape atom (lead INVALID)

invariant.name: lead.teth must be a twist, NULL, or UNIT; a known non-twist atom is provably wrong
invariant.spec: §4.2 Basic Body Shape p. 12 (teth must be twist, NULL, or UNIT); §9.5 lead INVALID
invariant.quote: lead INVALID: the lead's tether has a known non-twist shape. A teth atom of known shape (pairtrie, not twist) is structurally invalid for the slot.

moniker: R273: lead.teth shape byte 0xa0 (nospec upper range) → lead UNKNOWN

invariant.name: lead.teth has an unrecognized shape byte; the rig checker cannot classify it (lead UNKNOWN, yellow)
invariant.spec: §2.1 Identifiers p. 5 (unrecognized shape bytes are nospec); §9.5 lead UNKNOWN
invariant.quote: lead UNKNOWN: the lead's tether has an unrecognized shape. Shape byte 0xa0 is in a nospec range — the checker cannot determine whether it is a twist.

moniker: R297: intermediate twist between fastener and hoist is a proper hoist for some twist other than the declared meet (topline MISMATCH)

invariant.name: the topline walk must find no competing proper-hoist before reaching the declared hoist
invariant.spec: §7.3 Traversal Algorithm p. 38-46; §9.5 Hitch Structure Statuses topline
invariant.quote: If an intermediate twist on the topline is itself a proper hoist (incorporates the lead via shield function), the declared hoist is structurally ambiguous — topline MISMATCH, regardless of what the intermediate maps to.

moniker: R298: intermediate twist between fastener and hoist is itself a proper hoist for the same meet (topline MISMATCH)

invariant.name: when the topline walk encounters a proper hoist before reaching the declared hoist, the rig is structurally ambiguous
invariant.spec: §7.3 Traversal Algorithm p. 38-46; §9.5 Hitch Structure Statuses topline
invariant.quote: The traversal must identify the *first* twist along the topline that incorporates the lead via the shield function. If an intermediate twist already satisfies that condition, the declared hoist is not the canonical one — topline MISMATCH.

moniker: R299: intermediate twist between fastener and hoist is a proper hoist for a DIFFERENT (lead, meet) pair (topline MISMATCH)

invariant.name: when the topline walk encounters a twist that incorporates the lead but maps to a non-meet target, the rig is structurally ambiguous
invariant.spec: §7.3 Traversal Algorithm p. 38-46; §9.5 Hitch Structure Statuses topline
invariant.quote: The traversal must identify the first lead-incorporating twist on the topline. If an intermediate twist incorporates the lead but maps to a different target than the meet, the canonical hoist cannot be the declared one — topline MISMATCH.

moniker: R300: intermediate twist between fastener and hoist has body.rigs hash referencing an atom not in the bundle (topline-key MISSING)

invariant.name: a rigs hash with no matching atom is missing data, not provable invalidity
invariant.spec: §7.3 Traversal Algorithm p. 38-46; §9.5 Hitch Structure Statuses topline-key
invariant.quote: topline-key MISSING: there is no provided atom whose identifier matches the intermediate twist's body.rigs. Missing data is yellow.

moniker: R301: intermediate twist between fastener and hoist has body.rigs of non-trie shape (topline-key INVALID)

invariant.name: intermediate-twist body.rigs must be a trie or NULL; a known non-trie shape is provably invalid
invariant.spec: §4.2 Basic Body Shape p. 12; §3.1 Shape Errors p. 10; §9.5 topline-key
invariant.quote: topline-key INVALID: an intermediate twist on the topline has body.rigs of a known non-trie shape. The topline walk cannot examine the rigs trie.

moniker: R302: prev chain from hoist terminates with NULL before reaching fastener (topline MISMATCH)

invariant.name: topline walk from hoist must reach the fastener; a NULL prev partway is a structural MISMATCH
invariant.spec: §7.3 Traversal Algorithm p. 38-46; §9.5 Hitch Structure Statuses topline
invariant.quote: The topline is the prev-chain from hoist back to fastener. A NULL prev before reaching the fastener has a known non-fastener terminal — topline MISMATCH.

moniker: R304: prev chain from meet terminates with NULL before reaching lead (lead-footline MISMATCH)

invariant.name: lead-footline must terminate at the lead; a NULL prev partway is a structural MISMATCH
invariant.spec: §9.5 Hitch Structure Statuses, half-hitch p. 56-57; §7.3 Traversal p. 38-46
invariant.quote: half-hitch MISMATCH: the terminal twist of the lead-footline does not match the lead. A lead-footline whose prev chain reaches NULL before the lead has a known non-lead terminal — the failure is provably structural.

moniker: R305: prev chain from meet terminates on a non-twist atom before reaching lead (lead-footline INVALID)

invariant.name: lead-footline walk must follow twist prev links; a non-twist atom in prev is provably invalid
invariant.spec: §7.3 Traversal Algorithm p. 38-46; §9.5 Hitch Structure Statuses lead-footline
invariant.quote: lead-footline INVALID: the prev chain from meet encounters a non-twist atom. The body shape requires prev to be a twist or NULL; a known non-twist shape is a provable failure.

moniker: R306: prev chain from meet reaches another fast twist before reaching lead (half-hitch MISMATCH)

invariant.name: the lead-footline walk must reach the lead without encountering any other fast twist
invariant.spec: §7.3 Traversal Algorithm p. 38-46; §9.5 Hitch Structure Statuses half-hitch
invariant.quote: half-hitch MISMATCH: the prev chain from meet encounters a fast twist that is not the declared lead. The lead-footline must contain no internal fast points (only its endpoints).

moniker: R321: hitch post.rigs references a hash not present in the bundle (post-key MISSING)

invariant.name: a post.rigs hash with no matching atom is missing data, not provable invalidity
invariant.quote: post-key MISSING: there is no provided atom whose identifier matches post.rigs. Missing data is yellow under §error-handling.

moniker: R322: hitch post.rigs has a non-trie shape (arb instead of pairtrie)

invariant.name: post.rigs must be a trie or NULL; an arb-shaped atom in the rigs slot is a known-bad shape
invariant.spec: §3 Shapes p. 9-10; §4.2 Basic Body Shape p. 12; §9.5 Hitch Structure Statuses post-key
invariant.quote: rigs must be trie or null. An atom whose shape is in a known non-trie class (arb, hashes, etc.) is a provable shape error — the post-key cannot be resolved.

moniker: R323: hitch post.rigs is a valid pairtrie but contains no entry for lead (post-key MISMATCH)

invariant.name: post.rigs must contain a (lead → hoist) entry; absence is a structural MISMATCH
invariant.spec: §7.1 Splicing p. 32-34; §9.5 Hitch Structure Statuses post-key
invariant.quote: When a hitch has a post, post.rigs[lead] must equal the declared hoist. A pairtrie that lacks the lead key cannot satisfy this — post-key MISMATCH.

moniker: R324: hitch post.rigs[lead] maps to a hash that is not the declared hoist (post-key MISMATCH)

invariant.name: post.rigs[lead] must equal the declared hoist; a different target is a structural MISMATCH
invariant.spec: §7.1 Splicing p. 32-34; §9.5 Hitch Structure Statuses post-key
invariant.quote: post-key MISMATCH: post.rigs has an entry for lead, but the value does not match the declared hoist. The mapping is provably incorrect.

moniker: R51: lead.prev is a body-shape atom (body.prev shape error)

invariant.name: body.prev must be twist or NULL; a known non-twist shape in prev is a body-level shape error
invariant.spec: §4.2 Basic Body Shape p. 12 (prev: twist or NULL); §3.1 Shape Errors p. 10
invariant.quote: prev must be twist or NULL. A prev whose atom has body shape (0x49) is a known non-twist shape — provably invalid for the slot.

moniker: R53: lead.prev is an arb-shape atom (body.prev shape error)

invariant.name: body.prev must be twist or NULL; a known non-twist shape in prev is a body-level shape error
invariant.spec: §4.2 Basic Body Shape p. 12 (prev: twist or NULL); §3.1 Shape Errors p. 10
invariant.quote: prev must be twist or NULL. A prev whose atom has arb shape (0x60) is a known non-twist shape — provably invalid for the slot.

moniker: R53: lead.prev is a hashes-shape atom (body.prev shape error)

invariant.name: body.prev must be twist or NULL; a known non-twist shape in prev is a body-level shape error
invariant.spec: §4.2 Basic Body Shape p. 12 (prev: twist or NULL); §3.1 Shape Errors p. 10
invariant.quote: prev must be twist or NULL. A prev whose atom has hashes shape (0x61) is a known non-twist shape — provably invalid for the slot.

moniker: R53: lead.prev is a pairtrie-shape atom (body.prev shape error)

invariant.name: body.prev must be twist or NULL; a known non-twist shape in prev is a body-level shape error
invariant.spec: §4.2 Basic Body Shape p. 12 (prev: twist or NULL); §3.1 Shape Errors p. 10
invariant.quote: prev must be twist or NULL. A prev whose atom has pairtrie shape (0x63) is a known non-twist shape — provably invalid for the slot.

moniker: R56: lead body.prev is UNIT (alg=0xff); prev must be twist or NULL (shape error)

invariant.name: body.prev must be twist or NULL; UNIT is neither
invariant.spec: §2.1 Identifiers p. 5 (UNIT is the 1-byte 0xff terminator); §4.2 Basic Body Shape p. 12
invariant.quote: prev: previous twist; twist or NULL. UNIT (alg 0xff) is the sequence terminator, not a twist — a body whose prev slot is UNIT fails the body shape requirement.

moniker: Splice chain of four consecutive half-hitches sharing the corkline (hand-written)

invariant.name: splices may chain arbitrarily; the §7.3 traversal must extend forward through every consecutive hitch
invariant.quote: Splicing allows a rig to be extended in time. The traversal algorithm walks: hoist = verify_hitch(f_a); curr = f_a; next = hoist.rig[f_a]; ... while (hoist != FAIL) { ...; curr = next; hoist = verify_hitch(curr); ... }. The forward-extension loop must terminate cleanly and verify each step.

moniker: Tether loop in the rig (compiled from workshop rigs/29)

invariant.name: tether-loop avoided via depth cap → lash UNKNOWN (yellow) per §7.2
invariant.quote: Spec §7.2: 'a rig checker should return a failure upon detection of a tethering loop ... This failure shall be identified as a MISMATCH ... presented in either a lash or splice status object. The MISMATCH must only be used if there is a provable tether loop. If an implementation avoids following infinite loops by e.g. setting a height limit on rigs, a red error would be inappropriate, and the failure should be identified in a similar object as UNKNOWN.' The tether-loop here is on intermediate lines (vertical traversal up via tether), so the spec structype is `lash`, not `splice` (which is the focus-line walk).

moniker: Unit rig

invariant.name: the focus may be the corkline itself (no rig required)
invariant.quote: in a rig consisting of a single hitch, the topline functions as a corkline, and the footline as a leadline. When the focus already lies on the corkline, the rig degenerates to the unit case and passes trivially.

invariant.quote: in a rig consisting of a single hitch, the topline functions as a corkline, and the footline as a leadline. With more than one twist on the corkline, the traversal walks forward via successor links to locate the supporting endpoint.

moniker: Valid kiwano

invariant.name: end-to-end multi-line rig (focus = file's last twist)
invariant.quote: Reference end-to-end run of the §7.3 traversal algorithm: walk corkline, then verify_hitch chains down through intermediate lines until the leadline is supported.

moniker: Valid kiwano, variant 0

invariant.name: kiwano variant: alternate atom packing of the same logical rig
invariant.quote: Identifiers are self-describing in terms of their length, and can safely be concatenated one after the other ... A rig is verified independent of how its atoms are packed; a fresh ordering must still verify.

moniker: Valid kiwano, variant 1

invariant.name: kiwano variant: alternate atom packing of the same logical rig (second order)
invariant.quote: Identifiers are self-describing in terms of their length ... the verifier must produce the same answer regardless of which permutation of redundant atoms the file happens to contain.

moniker: Valid kiwano, f1

invariant.quote: The inputs expected by this rig traversal algorithm are: ... a leadline beginning f_a. The verifier must work for any chosen focus, not just the file's last twist.

moniker: Valid kiwano, f2

invariant.name: kiwano with focus = f2

moniker: Valid kiwano, f5

invariant.quote: Reference traversal example (Appendix B starts at f0 / f5 corners). The rig should verify when f_a corresponds to the deepest leadline twist supported by the corkline.
